sageBrowserModelHint: Works with quantized Llama 3 GGUF models. Everything runs on your own device — no cloud service, no account.
sageBrowserModelPublicHint: Public GGUF models from Hugging Face work without authentication. Only Llama 3 models are supported.
sageConfigDesc: These settings tune the experimental AI model the owl uses. AI is optional and runs on your device only.
sageContextPresetHint: Controls how much history and lesson evidence is sent to the model.
sageDynamicHello: 🦉 Experimental mode: ask about any topic in your tree. I use lesson content only.
sageAiOptionalBanner: Optional. Arborito works fully without AI — turn it on only if you want a hand.
sageExperimentalBuggyWarn: Heads-up: this preview can be slow or wrong. Guide mode (no AI) is still the recommended path.
sageExperimentalChatNote: Experimental AI is local: it processes your tree on-device without sending lessons to servers. You can switch back to Guide mode anytime.
sageExperimentalDisclaimer: Opt-in only — never turns itself on. The model runs on your device; nothing you type goes to any cloud service.
sageExperimentalStayGuide: No thanks — stay in Guide mode
sageGamesAiSettingsNote: Optional. Runs on your own device. Guide mode never uses AI.
sageGdprConnectsNote: One-time ~200 MB download from Hugging Face. Then everything runs locally. Remove it anytime from settings.
sageGdprText: If you turn it on, Arborito downloads an AI model once from Hugging Face and runs it locally — no cloud, no account.
sageGuideConstructNoSel: Tap a node on the map to edit it
sageGuideDiscoverLead: Pick a topic to read a short guide. You can step back one screen at a time or jump straight to the guide using the breadcrumbs above.
sageGuideSageIntro: Hi — I'm **Sage**, your guide inside Arborito. Tap any card below to find your way through the course or learn how the app works.
sageGuideSageIntroCon: **Construction mode.** I'll walk you through adding lessons, editing nodes and publishing your tree when it's ready.
sageGuideSageIntroLesson: You're inside a lesson. Tap a card below and I'll tell you **where you are**, **how to continue**, or whatever you need.
sageGuideTipEmpty:
The author of this lesson hasn't added extra information here yet.

• Once they do, it will appear in this same panel.
• In the meantime, you can head back to the **map** with 🏠 and continue with another lesson.
sageGuideTreeEmpty: Explore the map to discover content.
sageHello: 🦉 Hoot hoot! I am awake. Ask me anything.
sageLoadingBrainDesc: Downloading the AI model to your browser. This usually happens only once.
sageMaxNewTokensHint: Lower = faster and shorter answers; higher = longer replies.
sageMenuChatDesc: Ask the owl helper about this lesson.
sageModeAiTooltip: Optional AI mode (you turn it on separately).
sageModeGuideTooltip: No AI: only text the author already wrote in the lesson.
sageQuickQuiz: Ask me a practice question from this lesson.
sageQuizEmpty:
This lesson doesn't have a questionnaire yet.

• That's normal — not every lesson includes one.
• When one is added, you'll be able to review it from the **Arcade** with minigames built from its questions.
sageQuizStatusHint: Heads up: I don't show answers here, to avoid spoilers. The review is solved on the lesson page itself.
sageQuizStatusIncomplete:
**The review is started but incomplete.**

• The author left some fields unfinished, so a few questions or hints may be missing.
• You can still scroll down on the lesson and practice what's there.
sageQuizStatusNone:
**This lesson has no review.**

• When you finish reading, head back to the map with **🏠** and pick another lesson.
• If you want to drill the whole tree, open the **Arcade** from the dock.
sageQuizStatusReady:
**The review is ready for you to solve.**

• Scroll down on the lesson page to the review section.
• Answer it right there — your progress is saved locally on this device.
• If you'd rather drill with minigames, open **«Practice in the Arcade»** from this panel.
sageSectionQuizIncomplete: There's a review started, but it's not complete yet.
sageSettingsBrowserDesc: Runs entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. Data stays on this device.
sageSettingsDesktopDesc: Runs natively on your machine. Uses your CPU and, when available, GPU (Metal / CUDA / Vulkan) for maximum performance. The model file and settings are stored on this device.
sageSupportBackMap:
**Back to the map** takes you to the course's tree.

• Tap **🏠** in the bottom dock or the Arborito logo at the top.
• You can also close this panel with the **×** or by swiping down.
• Once on the map, tap any circle to open another lesson.
sageSupportContinue:
You're on **«{name}»**. A few ideas for what to do next:

• If this lesson has a **review at the bottom**, finish it before moving on — it locks in what you just read.
• Want to drill with minigames? Tap **«Practice in the Arcade»** from this panel.
• To switch topic, head back to the map with **🏠** and tap another circle on the tree.
• Completing **every lesson in a module** earns you a seed 🌱 — they collect in your backpack.

Tip: there's no fixed order, so feel free to explore the topics that interest you most.
sageSupportLessonHelp:
Each **lesson** is a page on the map (the «leaf» circles). Here's how they work:

• The actual content lives on the **page**, not in this panel — I only help you find your way around.
• If there's a **review**, it sits at the bottom of the lesson. Answer it there to fix what you learned.
• Mark the lesson as **«Completed»** when you finish reading and reviewing it.
• Completing every lesson in a module earns a **seed 🌱**, which you'll see in your backpack along with your streak and XP.

If you get lost, tap **«Where am I?»** above in this panel and I'll show you where you are in the tree.
sageSupportWhere:
You're reading **«{name}»**.

**Path in the tree**
{path}

To switch topic:

• Tap **🏠** in the dock to return to the map and pick another circle.
• Or open the **side index** and browse modules.
• To search by name, use **«Search»** from the menu or from Sage's panel.
sageTopicConAddB1: Tap a module on the map and press + (bottom bar on mobile).
sageTopicConAddB2: Pick module, lesson or exam and name it.
sageTopicConAddB3: You can reorder nodes by dragging afterward.
sageTopicConAddLead: Building a course is like planting: drop a module, then add leaves (lessons) or exams to it.
sageTopicConAddS1b: Tap the parent module on the map. On mobile, use the + button in the dock; on desktop, right-click to open the menu.
sageTopicConAddS2b: You can create a module (another branch), a lesson (text + optional quiz) or an exam (lesson with required quiz).
sageTopicConAddS3b: Type a clear name and save. The node appears on the map right away and is ready to edit.
sageTopicConAddS4b: Drag any node onto another branch to reorganize. The tree rearranges itself.
sageTopicConEditB1: Context menu: properties, move, duplicate or delete.
sageTopicConEditB2: Leaves open the lesson and quiz editor.
sageTopicConEditLead: Every node is edited separately. Leaves also have a rich text editor and an optional quiz that feeds the Arcade.
sageTopicConEditS1b: Tap a circle (module) or a leaf to select it. You'll see quick actions: edit, move, duplicate or delete.
sageTopicConEditS2b: Rename, change type or tweak visibility. Changes show up on the map immediately.
sageTopicConEditS3b: On a lesson, Open takes you to the rich editor: paste markdown, images and code blocks.
sageTopicConEditS4b: Questions feed the Arcade minigames. The more you add, the better your readers can review.
sageTopicConPubB1: Before publishing: fill the “About this tree” card (description, minimum 5 characters) on top of the map and save it. Without that you can't publish.
sageTopicConPubB2: Every change saves instantly on your device. Publish shares the tree with others (top or bottom bar).
sageTopicConPubB3: You can undo or revert to a saved version at any time.
sageTopicConPubLead: Publishing shares your tree with the network. First, complete the course card so it presents well.
sageTopicConPubS1b: Open “About this tree” above the map. Set the title, description (at least 5 chars) and other basic details.
sageTopicConPubS2b: Every change is saved on this device instantly. You can undo or revert to earlier versions.
sageTopicConPubS3b: Press Publish (top or bottom bar) to upload the tree to the decentralized network. It will appear in other people's directory.
sageTopicConPubS4b: Whenever you change something important, publish again. Readers receive the new version next time they open the course.
sageTopicGardenB1: Seeds 🌱 mark progress when you complete modules.
sageTopicGardenB2: Your streak rewards studying a little each day.
sageTopicGardenB3: Your backpack suggests what to review based on what you read.
sageTopicGardenLead: Arborito gamifies study without pressure: seeds, streak and smart review.
sageTopicGardenS1b: Complete modules to earn seeds. They reflect real progress in the course.
sageTopicGardenS2b: Studying a little each day keeps your streak. No marathon required.
sageTopicGardenS3b: Your backpack suggests what to review based on what you read and game results.
sageTopicIntroB1: Courses as trees: branches (topics) and leaves (lessons). You choose the path.
sageTopicIntroB2: Progress stays on this device; study at your own pace.
sageTopicIntroB3: Arcade mini-games review when a lesson has a quiz.
sageTopicIntroLead: A forest of knowledge: each course is a tree with branches (topics) and leaves (lessons). You choose the path.
sageTopicIntroS1b: Pan, zoom and open any node. No rigid list — explore like a game.
sageTopicIntroS2b: Each leaf has text, author notes and sometimes a quiz to review.
sageTopicIntroS3b: Mini-games built from the author's questions. Best after reading.
sageTopicIntroS4b: Your progress lives in this browser. You can sync or publish trees to share.
sageTopicIntroS5b: Construction mode lets you build whole courses in the UI: modules, lessons and publishing.
sageTopicNavB1: Swipe or drag to pan; pinch or wheel to zoom.
sageTopicNavB2: Tap a circle (module) to expand; a leaf opens the lesson.
sageTopicNavB3: The 🏠 dock button recenters the map.
sageTopicNavLead: The tree is interactive: explore freely without losing your place.
sageTopicNavS1b: On mobile, one finger pans. On desktop, click-drag or the mouse wheel.
sageTopicNavS2b: Pinch on mobile or wheel + Ctrl on desktop. The 🏠 button recenters the tree.
sageTopicNavS3b: Tap a circle (module) to expand. Tap a leaf to read the lesson.
sageTopicNavS4b: The bottom dock (mobile) or sidebar (desktop) gives quick access to the map.
sageTopicStudyB1: Tap a leaf on the map to read; no fixed order required.
sageTopicStudyB2: In each lesson, Sage shows where you are and what to do next.
sageTopicStudyB3: Review in Arcade or from your backpack anytime.
sageTopicStudyLead: You don't need to follow everything in order. Pick whichever topic calls you and go deep at your own pace.
sageTopicStudyS1b: The tree doesn't force a path. Start from a topic you care about and let the connections lead you.
sageTopicStudyS2b: Each leaf is a lesson with text, examples and notes. Read without a clock; your progress saves itself.
sageTopicStudyS3b: Inside a lesson, Sage tells you where you are, what to do next and surfaces extra notes when they exist.
sageTopicStudyS4b: If the lesson has a quiz, open the Arcade to review with minigames built from those questions.
sageTopicStudyS5b: The backpack summarizes your progress: seeds earned, daily streak and topics worth reviewing soon.
